title: Markdown reference
summary: Reference for the Markdown syntax and extensions supported by tinymash.

sections.title: Supported baseline
sections.markdown: tinymash renders CommonMark with the GitHub-flavored features already in use in the composer. That includes tables, task lists, strikethrough, fenced code blocks, and inline code.

sections.title: Headings
sections.markdown:
Use `#` through `######` for `H1` through `H6`.

```md
# H1
## H2
### H3
```

sections.title: Text styles
sections.markdown:
- Bold: `**bold**`
- Italic: `*italic*`
- Underline: `++underlined++`
- Strikethrough: `~~strikethrough~~`
- Highlight: `==highlight==`
- Inline code: `` `inline code` ``

Underline renders as semantic `<ins>`, not as bold. Only the exact `++text++` delimiter pair is treated as underline.

sections.title: Quotes and callouts
sections.markdown:
```md
> A normal quote

> [!INFO]
> An info alert

> [!SUCCESS]
> A success alert

> [!WARNING]
> A warning alert

> [!WARNING|Important]
> A warning alert with a custom visible label

> [!DANGER]
> A danger alert
```

The editor alert menu inserts Bootstrap-style alert markers. Supported variants are `PRIMARY`, `SECONDARY`, `SUCCESS`, `DANGER`, `WARNING`, `INFO`, `LIGHT`, and `DARK`.

You can also override the visible label with `|Custom label`, for example `> [!WARNING|Important]`.

Legacy aliases still work: `NOTE` = `INFO`, `TIP` = `SUCCESS`, `IMPORTANT` = `PRIMARY`, and `CAUTION` = `DANGER`.

sections.title: Lists and tasks
sections.markdown:
```md
- One
- Two

1. First
2. Second

- [ ] Review
- [x] Publish
```

sections.title: Blank lines
sections.markdown: A normal blank line separates blocks. Extra blank lines between blocks are preserved as visible vertical spacing in public rendering. Blank lines inside fenced code blocks stay part of the code block.

sections.title: Tables
sections.markdown:
Tables need a header row and a separator row.

```md
| Name | Value |
| --- | --- |
| SPF | pass |
| DKIM | pass |
```

sections.title: Code blocks
sections.markdown:
Use fenced code blocks, ideally with a language when you want syntax highlighting.

~~~md
```php
<?php echo 'Hello';
```
~~~

Tilde fences such as `~~~php` also work.

sections.title: Emoji shortcodes
sections.markdown:
tinymash understands GitHub-style emoji shortcodes, including common values such as:

```md
:blush: :smile: :grin: :wink: :heart:
:thumbsup: :thumbsdown: :rocket:
:fire: :sparkles: :warning:
:white_check_mark: :x: :memo: :bulb:
```

The composer can autocomplete shortcodes while you type after `:`.

sections.title: Traditional smileys
sections.markdown:
tinymash can also convert classic smileys during rendering outside code blocks, for example:

```md
:) :-) :( :-( ;) ;-) :D :-D :P :-P :/ :-/ :\ :-\ 8) 8-) :| :-| <3
```

This behavior follows the site default and can also be overridden per profile.

sections.title: What does not get converted
sections.markdown: Emoji shortcodes and smileys are intentionally left alone inside inline code and fenced code blocks so examples stay literal.
